seoTitle: How to Export Jira Issues to Excel
metaDescription: Export Jira issues to an Excel file so you can share, analyze, or archive your project data outside of Jira.
intro: Sometimes you need Jira data in spreadsheet form, whether for a stakeholder report or offline analysis. Jira has a built-in way to export a set of issues straight to Excel. This guide walks through doing it correctly.

steps.heading: Search for the issues you need
steps.body: Use Jira's issue search or a saved filter to pull together the exact set of issues you want in the export.

steps.heading: Open the export menu
steps.body: From the search results screen, find the export option, usually located near the top of the issue list.

steps.heading: Choose the Excel export format
steps.body: Select the Excel (CSV or XLSX) option rather than other formats like Word or PDF.

steps.heading: Select which fields to include
steps.body: Pick the fields you want in the spreadsheet, such as summary, status, assignee, and due date.

steps.heading: Download and open the file
steps.body: Download the generated file and open it in Excel or another spreadsheet tool to review the data.

faq.question: Is there a limit to how many issues I can export at once?
faq.answer: Jira typically caps large exports, so very big projects may need to be exported in smaller batches.

faq.question: Can I customize which columns appear in the export?
faq.answer: Yes, the export usually lets you choose or reorder fields before downloading the file.

faq.question: Does exporting to Excel remove the data from Jira?
faq.answer: No, exporting only creates a copy; the original issues remain untouched in Jira.

faq.question: Can I automate this export on a schedule?
faq.answer: Native exports are manual, though some Jira apps or the API can be used to automate recurring exports.
